Narrative
Easily accessible via the scenic, well-paved Tanay
Sitio Masalat Sampaloc-Pililla roads, this serene campsite sits atop a hill, offering a peaceful, windy retreat—often graced with fog and a refreshing chill. The expansive grounds are blanketed with lush, carabao grass and shaded by hundreds of mango trees, creating the perfect setting for relaxation and outdoor activities.
Facilities include ample restrooms, baths, and a washing bay. Early mornings are ideal for family or group activities such as walking, jogging, biking, or even riding to the Pililla wind turbines just a kilometer away. Spanning 7 hectares of orchard farmland, the site offers a rejuvenating, nature-filled escape. Phase II extends across another 7 hectares, featuring mahogany, narra, and coconut trees, with an elevated vantage point overlooking Laguna de Bay.
Many campers take to the skies with drones, capturing breathtaking views of the campsite and the stunning landscape of Tanay Windmills Viewpoint.
A favorite hub for bikers and riders, the campsite carefully regulates the number of visitors to preserve its peaceful and orderly atmosphere. The on-site cafeteria offers internet access, freshly brewed Batangas coffee, and a delightful all-day breakfast menu—making it the perfect spot to unwind and enjoy the view. The camp is also pet-friendly!
Come and experience the beauty, adventure, and tranquility of Tanay Windmills Viewpoint Café!
